steps.title: Identify what to look for
steps.content: The question asks which of the four listed classes ($2002$, $2004$, $2005$, $2007$) had the fewest graduates enrolled in college within 24 months. Compare the heights of just those four points on the line graph and pick the lowest one.

steps.title: Compare the four candidate years from the figure
steps.content: Reading the heights of the four candidate points on the line graph, the point above $2002$ sits lower than the points above $2004$, $2005$, and $2007$. That makes $2002$ the smallest of the four listed years.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A. 2002 — lowest of the four candidate points on the graph. Correct.
  • B. 2004 — the point above $2004$ is higher than the point above $2002$, so not the fewest.
  • C. 2005 — the point above $2005$ is higher than the point above $2002$, so not the fewest.
  • D. 2007 — the point above $2007$ is higher than the point above $2002$, so not the fewest.
